text:
Dear Fiancé, Here Are 10 Things I Won't Change About Myself After Getting Married!
I'm told that wives have to behave appropriately but I'm sorry. I absolutely cannot promise that I won't ambush you with hugs and kisses no matter where we are. So be prepared.
What is this growing up business? I can't handle all this. I'm an extremely passionate person who gets excited about little things. I will drag you for movies I'm dying to watch, make elaborate date night plans, and will continue to make a big deal out of birthdays and anniversaries. That's just how it is.
Capture your special moments in memorable photo frames available on Amazon for Rs 175.
There cannot be a bigger hint for you regarding what makes me happy. I love random surprises. So feel free to buy me flowers or pamper me with an impromptu trip! Okay? Okay!
My family and friends hold a huge, irreplaceable place in my heart and my life. And to be totally honest, I wouldn't change it for the world. I can't wait for you to get to know everyone and be an even bigger part of my world.
Make sure you are well organized at work and in life with the help of this diary available on Amazon for Rs 249.
I don't know if you know this yet, but I've been told my eyes sparkle every time I look at you. I absolutely love the dimension you've added to my life and I promise to always cherish that. I promise to always look at you like maybe you are magic.
I know that you are a progressive man and we have discussed how we both love what we do. I promise to stay as motivated and passionate about my work as you are and absolutely know we are both going to kill it in life!
A calming massage session after a hard day at work? Why not? Get this lavender infused massage oil on Nykaa for Rs 280 only!
Come on, you know this about me! I am addicted to TV. And we all know this will come up at some point so let's just discuss it now. You are going to have to compromise your TV privileges, because you are about to marry an addict. Okay, good that that's out of the way.
8. I will not change how I feel about game night!
I am super competitive and won't change that about myself just because we are getting married. 'game night' means each man for himself. Oh, and I absolutely LOVE winning.
Popcorns are mandatory during game night, right? Get your pack of yummy chocolate flavoured popcorn on Amazon for Rs 189.
You know I'm the kind of person who always keeps it real. Just because we are getting married, I will not suddenly become politically correct. That's right. With me, you'll always get 100% authentic and honest. Promise!
Okay, maybe this one isn't all that true. Because the love I have for you only keeps growing. I guess that means that it'll change, right?
Surprise him with a fabulously cooked homemade meal with the help of this cookbook. Get it on Amazon for Rs 379.

In my project I have a Unity WebGL build running from the content collection. There is also a .txt file in the content collection that I am using to hold specific set up information about the project, and I can successfully access the .txt file using www requests. The goal is to have an administrator section of my game where the instructor could view and modify the data stored in the .txt file with minimal knowledge of the system. This is important because it lets the teacher tailor the game to the needs of the class with minimal effort. I know that the correct way to do this is using Blackboard learn web services to set up a web request that will then write the data to the content collection, but due to certain restrictions (time, money, my lack of experience with servers) this route is a last option.
I think that there is a better architecture for your project based in the LTI standard.
You will develop a standalone web application known as LTI Provider, which provides your game in an iframe or a new window, (where your game will live). In Blackboard Learn you will provide a link to your application as a content or a tool, and when a user clicks the link a request to your server will be performed. This request will contain all the authentication and context information your application needs to be sure that the user is logged, who is he, which course, content and institution is in, which role he has in the course and other details. With this information your application will be able to show the scene for the administrator if he is an instructor, keep in a database or xls file the progress of the user or even, using the LTI Outcomes services, send grades to the Blackboard Learn Grades Center.
You can combine this technologies with the Blackboard Learn REST API if you need a higher integration level, but with the LTI standard you'll have most of what you will need. You will be able of performing updates and maintenance in the LTI Provider in a transparent way, because the user has not contents in their LMS, all the assets, dbs and files are centralised in your own server, they only have a link to your app with the required credentials.
Update: Thank you Alberto Ruiz for your suggestion. I agree that using an LTI Provider would be the best solution, but my client refuses to give me BlackBoard administrator rights, and doesn't want to manage any external servers. Because of this here is my cumbersome Rube Goldberg solution.
This was not clear in my initial description, but the end goal was to load pictures from the content collection, along with information about the images provided in a config file.
Within my Unity project I created a streaming assets folder that holds all the images we want to load, along with a config file. Using Application.streamingAssetsPath I am able to load the config file that has the information about each picture, and the url to the image we need to load. After the application loads this config is read, and each image is loaded into the game.
Allowing the instructor to edit the config was not possible, but I found another solution. First the instructor uploads all the images they want to the StreamingAssets folder in the content collection. Next the instructor launches the game and access a special admin section allowing them to edit the config data. Initially the instructor had to type in the exact url of the image they wanted to import into the game, but that was cruel and unusual. So I created a HTML parser that would grab the HTML of the streaming assets folder and search for links to additional folders and images, recursively searching in found folders. The data gathered from the HTML parser is used to display a file browser that mimics the structure of the content collection, where clicking on an image element loads that file from the content collection. After the user is finished editing the config they can download it, and replace the current one in the streaming assets folder.

Pink Polka Dot Books: All the Missing Girls by Megan Miranda-- Adult Mystery w/ YA Crossover Appeal!!
All the Missing Girls by Megan Miranda-- Adult Mystery w/ YA Crossover Appeal!!
When Nic returns to her small town 10 years after her high school best friend disappeared, she finds herself caught in the center of another girl's disappearance.
In order to find out what happened to Annaliese, Nic needs to find out the truth about her friend Corrinne. And this truth involves her family, her friends, and everything she's been running from for the last 10 years.
Nicolette comes to town to pack up her childhood home because her dad can't afford to have the house and pay for his care. She rarely ever comes back because of the painful memories-- mainly due to her best friend going missing and it never being solved. Within days of returning ANOTHER girl goes missing-- and it's once again tied to her friend/family group. The girl that's missing is dating Nic's high school ex and it turns out she was obsessed with the Corrine disappearance.
Interesting thing about this book: It's told backward. It starts out with a short introduction and then we jump to 2 weeks into the future and work our way back. You would think that reading a Mystery backward would not work-- I mean, that's what I was thinking at first-- but I was wrong. Apparently, you can totally start at the end and go backward and still somehow still feel like you are moving forward TOWARDS something??? Well, this author can do that for you anyway.
You know what will get me every time? The enigmatic, addicting, charismatic, best friend. Corrine is missing before the book starts, but she's larger than life on these pages. Every time there is a Corrine in a book, I find myself drawn in. Why do I like a character that is mysterious and equal parts alluring & dangerous?? I have no idea. It's not like these characters are really ever likable. They're the ones using the nice girl's secrets against her. They're the ones pulling all the strings-- and when something bad happens to them, I think we're supposed to feel like they had it coming? All I ever feel is that I wish I knew them too.
The other cast of characters in this book weren't bad either. From the brother and his wife to the ex-boyfriends and the neighbor. I liked getting to know and suspect them all. The part that really threw me was the father. He's in a nursing home and has some sort of dementia, but he KNOWS something. That part of the mystery I loved trying to unravel. It made the book all the more complicated and muddled.
Recently I've been reading a lot of Adult Mystery/Thrillers, and I have to say if I find more like this one, it may become a habit. There are flashbacks to high school days, and that brought the YA aspects in that I personally like to read about. Megan Miranda was already cemented as one of my fave authors-- and now she's proving me right every time!!
OVERALL: YES!! I loved this backward mystery!! It's told from Day 15 to Day 1 and it is done so well. I will recommend this to both Adult and YA readers because I think it has some crossover appeal. Put Megan Miranda on your auto-buy list and you won't be sorry.
Nicolette is a fan of "starting over" and I could totally see her using different variations of her name in the different towns she's lived in.
I LOVED this one. It being told backwards was so different and thought it really worked. Glad you loved it too!
It was really cool the way it worked out. At first I was totally confused how a mystery could work backwards, but somehow she pulled it off. Have you read any of her other books?
I keep picking this one up. I am glad to hear that you liked it. I had no idea it was told backwards. Makes it even more interesting to me. I may have to pick it up at some point.
WOO!! I totally recommend!! The backwards story really made the book.
I find the backward approach interesting, and glad it worked for you. There is something about knowing the endpoint, and then trying to figure out how the characters got there.

So I a had run planned for this morning, but woke up to more rain. I was feeling energized and ready to go so I was kinda bummed about the weather. Around that time I was reminded.."It's not acid..it's just rain". That was all I needed.
Several layers later I was at the track. My ipod was fully charged this time and it really does make a ton of difference. I can probably make every song lyric apply to my life in some way, but it seemed like my ipod was talking to me this morning.
The bad news is the track here in Milner is kinda old, so there were parts that were completely flooded with water. I walked around them for the first 10 minutes. The second walk/run 10 minutes were another story. I stopped worrying about the water and just ran. Within seconds my feet were soaked up to my knees. I also got hot pretty quickly, so several layers had to come off.
The good news?? I did it. I met this week's goal on day 2. During my middle 10 I ran for 6 minutes and walked for 4 (alternating every two minutes). I was surprised how quickly 2 minutes went by. While I am proud of myself for being able to do it, I'm not moving to goal 2 yet. I'm going to stick with the same routine the rest of the week with the new goal being for it to be a lil easier to do. It was rough. It doesn't seem like a lot of running, but it was for me. By the 3rd round of running I was hurting. I really do think the rain helped though. It made me feel free and energized.
Glad you had someone there to remind you that running in the rain is amazing. Was anyone else out there? Doubt it! That was all you babe! Good on ya!
Aww! I am proud of you and your new goal of running. It is nice to see you blog as well.

VIDEO: This video clip is large and slow loading. Click on it now, and allow it to load while you study the techniques below. The video will play each of the fills below, in sequence.
Notice: This is much easier than it looks, if you know your paradiddles. Simply place your right hand (stick) on one drum and the left on another. Play a full measure of 16th paradiddles then return to the beat.
This sound file . . . adapts 4th (Quarter) triplets to shuffle. Notice that since this is an easy fill to coordinate, we are playing simultaneous Quarter triplets with both hands. Experiment!!! 4th-note triplets may be used with almost any beat pattern (ie: Funk, Fatback, 4th Rock & others).
This sound file . . . adapts 16th triplets to shuffle. 16th-note triplets may be used with almost any beat pattern (ie: Funk, Fatback, 4th Rock & others) if the tempo is slow enough. REMEMBER! You are only human! There ARE limits here!
This sound file . . . adapts the 4 stroke ruff to shuffle. Sometimes called the 'Rogers Ruff', it may be used with almost any beat pattern too (ie: Funk, Fatback, 4th Rock & others) if the tempo is slow enough. REMEMBER! You are only human! There ARE limits here!
Mix and match combinations are fun and easy. Think of the possibilities! Try a half measure of singles followed by a half measure of Paradiddles or Eighth Triplets. The possible configurations are nearly infinite. The more roll types you know how to play, the more combination possibilities exist.
No sound file! . . . My poor little drum machine midi file maker laid down and died on this! Score one for us lowly humanoids! This is a simple example of the human imagination at work. Can you see the billions of ways these roll patterns may be configured to create countless fill possibilities? The rhythmic variables are virtually endless!
Check out E-M lesson #5. It is designed to you how to create your own innovative fills.

Rosewill RC-202 Internal SATA to eSATA II External 2Port Host Bracket Cable 11"
When connecting the drive again, you may also need to re-scan your drives.
Pros: Inexpensive and it works fine.
Cons: Described length is mis-leading. It's 11"... only if you measure from the external faceplace to the tip of the internal eSATA connector. Actual cable length is 8". In reality, if you're motherboard eSATA connector is more than 7" from the rear to your case, you'll need a longer part number than this.
Other Thoughts: Should read the reviews before buying. My case-2-connector distance is just under 9". Thought I had an extra 2" of slack to play with. Shipping costs more to send this back. Oh well.
Other Thoughts: really really nice product, would buy another if needed.
Pros: Works and is cheap.
Other Thoughts: It's basically a pass through SATA to eSATA (female) cable. Bracket cleans up the look at the back of your case. So it just works.
But from memory I remember not finding anything really that long with brackets. While length did worry me somewhat before purchase it turns out to work great for me.
My MB came with 8 SATA ports. 4 used for internal drives and the other 4 I used specifically with these brackets. But I did use the SATA ports closer to the back of the case/MB.
For what it's worth it would not have reach the front SATA ports if I wanted to used those instead.
So if your SATA ports is anywhere pass the halfway point of your MB I would worry a bit about length.
Pros: Looks like it would work okay.
Cons: The cables are just a bit too short to plug in to my SATA connectors from where it's supposed to be mounted on the PCI slot on the back of the tower. Perhaps having an L shape connector, it might have been able to squeeze it in.
Other Thoughts: It's always better to have more cable length.
Other Thoughts: I installed this in my Dell Dimension 9200 and the cables were plenty long enough. After installing I hooked up the CAVALRY CAXB3701T0 1TB external drive using eSATA to eSATA cable and enjoyed lightening fast response times!

So I completed like over half of the challenges.
So I think I'm going to say I was successful in this thing.
This completes the 'A book with red on the cover' & 'A book from the genre you've read the least this year' challenges, as it's blatantly red and horror fiction. Oh my god, was it gory. Far more than the first, which was a surprise that I should have expected. I mainly read this for Will Henry, also known as one of the best sidekicks ever. This kid is getting good character development.
This fulfills no quotas, but it was short and interesting (and super creepy) so I read it and enjoyed it. I had to do a little double-take at the end. Seriously, that ending. What.
While it definitely counts for 'A book with pictures', I'm also the including 'A book-to-movie adaptation' challenge, even if it's not technically true. And I watched it two years ago, not during the book-tube-a-thon. But details, details, I'm counting it.
It was short and cute and really clever, so it was perfect for this booktube-a-thon thing.
Which was also amazing. I was then going to go on and read The Way of Kings while I was on this Sanderson kick, but I saw how long it was and got intimidated. Over 1000 pages. Yikes.
I had nearly finished this book before the booktube-a-thon, so I decided to read it to fulfill the 'read seven books' challenge. Honestly, I didn't like it very much, especially after reading two really fabulous fantasy novels.
I also read about one third of 'if nobody speaks of remarkable things' by Jon McGregor, but I was lazy so I didn't finish it. And I read about fifty pages of Birdsong, which was inbetween this whole fantasy thing I was doing near the end of the week, and I realised that this wasn't the genre I wanted to be reading.
If I calculate my page count (Which really isn't very high because I'd started a lot of these books beforehand) is about: 1750 pages.
So I completed most of the challenges, and I read a book a day, and I'm really happy with that. This was surprisingly fun.
I would recommend that you guys try this when it rolls around again, because it gives a real sense of achievement when you can tick off a challenge, and if you watch the youtube videos along with it, there's a very nice sense of community.
Yeah, I'll have to do this again next year.
I'm bored and need incentive to read more instead of wasting all my time on tumblr.
And I'm probably not going to complete all the challenges. But let's go.
1. The Avengers Prequel: Fury's Big Week - This has been sitting on my shelf since this avengers movie came out... two years ago? And it has pictures. So there.
Start and finish a series.
2. The Final Empire by Brandon Sanderson - I was already reading this one by the time booktubeathon rolled around so this works by shear coincidence. But I don't have the next two books, so we're cheating this challenge.
A book with red on the cover & A book from the genre you've read the least this year.
3. The Curse of the Wendigo by Rick Yancey - I haven't read any horror/middle-grade (is this middle-grade?) this year, and I really enjoy this series, even if it is extremely gory.
A book someone else picks out for you.
4. if nobody speaks of remarkable things by Jon McGregor - Recommended to me by a friend, which so totally counts.
5. Birdsong by Sebastian Faulks - I can't exactly watch the movie adaptation yet because it doesn't exist, but it's in the works, so it technically will have an adaptation at some point.
This list will probably change, since I can never stick to TBRs, but hopefully this will offer some motivation to get some reading done this week. Or what remains of this week.

This is a simple comparison. Enjoy.
I have always wanted to see a comparison but always forget to shoot with my phone during a shoot. But not this time!!! Phone images are taken by Julia's loving father, David Mays. Julia is my Senior from last night. We shot at the Folk School. LOVE that place. I shot this session with two Nikon D750 DSLR cameras with a 24mm 1.8 lens and a 50mm 1.8 lens. Edits are in Lightroom with LXC 04 C preset with tweeks to fix white balance. All images are shot with camera and sunlight, no flash or reflectors, and the metering mode is set to shoot for the highlights (thank you Sean Carr, Twyla Jones and Brooke Allen for teaching me this.) We did have Julia's father close by to help with laughter and wrapping Julia in her scarf that she made.
Want to talk more about photography? I'd love to!
Want me to be your photographer? I'd love to!
Want to just hang out? I'd love to!
And these are more of my favorites. Just because.
